hypothesis: If pretrained ML surrogates (eqV2 or eSEN) are used to obtain ML-only minimum energy pathways and DFT SSNEB is restarted from those ML-converged images, then the ML-SSNEB workflow will substantially reduce the number of DFT iterations and total computational time compared to VASP-only SSNEB while producing converged MEPs and transition states (energies, forces, and geometries) that agree with VASP-only results under the same convergence criterion (forces < 0.01 eV/Å) for CsPbI3 and TiO2.
